nodes.label: P03_T017
nodes.identity_summary: This is a professionally sorted, personally growing urban office worker who is highly pragmatic and research-driven. They prioritize fragrance longevity and budget, but are willing to pay a premium for clinically or Ayurvedically validated ingredients. They distrust influencer hype, rely heavily on online reviews and dermatologist opinions, and make final purchase decisions based on a calculated balance of price, proven ingredients, and peer validation.

nodes.label: Context-Aware Balancer
nodes.dimension: Emotional versus Rational Advertising Message Processing

nodes.label: Content-Focused Consistent
nodes.dimension: Contextual Sensitivity to Ad Exposure Environment

nodes.label: Balanced Evidence-Seeker
nodes.dimension: Holistic versus Analytical Product Evaluation Style

nodes.label: price-first-then-reviews-purchaser
nodes.description: Explicitly ranks 'price' as the #1 factor when buying personal care, followed by 'fragrance' and then 'reviews'. This ordering dictates their initial screening of products.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] When buying any personal care product, rank these factors from most to least important: A: ["price","fragrance","reviews","recommendation","brand","ingredients","packaging","expert"]
[REAL] When shopping online for personal care, what do you look at first? Pick your top 3: A: ["star_rating","review_count","price"]
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a premium-priced body wash concept, this person would likely dismiss it unless it first proves exceptional value and review scores.

nodes.label: fragrance-longevity-maximizer
nodes.description: Seeks body wash fragrance that lasts 'All_day' and has switched products due to 'poor longevity' and 'fragrance too weak'. This is a primary driver of dissatisfaction.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How long should a body wash fragrance last on your skin? A: All_day
[REAL] Name a specific product whose fragrance you loved... Didn't repurchase – poor longevity, fragrance too weak.
[REAL] What would make you switch away from your current body wash? Pick your top 3: A: ["better_smell","ad_resonance"]
nodes.predictive_value: When shown an ad claiming '72hr_freshness', this person would be highly attentive and likely to click for reviews to verify the claim.

nodes.label: ayurvedic-ingredients-validator
nodes.description: Defines 'natural' through a specific, process-oriented lens: 'Made from Ayurvedic herbs', 'Separate machinery', 'Non-chemical raw elements', and seeks certifications like 'Ayurvedic'.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Could you share what ingredients or qualities you specifically look for that make a product feel 'natural' to you? A: 1. Made from Ayurvedic herbs 2. Separate machinery for processing each product 3. Non-chemical raw elements 4. Chemicals used within limits
[REAL] Which certifications or labels influence your purchase? Select all that apply. A: Sulfate_free,organic,ayurvedic
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a product page with an AYUSH certification, they would spend extra time verifying the claim and checking for matching ingredients.

nodes.label: dermatologist-trust-maximizer
nodes.description: Rates dermatologists as the most trusted source (5/5) for product information, far above influencers, friends, or brand claims.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How much do you trust the following sources for personal care product information? Rate each 1-5: A: {"TV advertisements":2,"Instagram or YouTube influencers":4,"Dermatologists or skin doctors":5,"Friends and family":4,"Online reviews (Amazon, Nykaa)":4,"Claims written on the product packaging":1}
nodes.predictive_value: A video ad featuring a board-certified dermatologist explaining a pH-balanced formula would significantly increase their purchase intent compared to a lifestyle influencer ad.

nodes.label: influencer-hype-skeptic
nodes.description: Distrusts influencers who 'push products forcefully for likes and views' and has never bought a product directly from social media.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Is there something specific about seeing products on Instagram or YouTube that makes you hesitate to purchase them directly from there? A: Influencers push products forcefully for likes and views
[REAL] Have you ever bought a personal care product because you saw it on social media? Describe what happened. A: No, never
nodes.predictive_value: A high-production influencer campaign with millions of followers would be met with suspicion; they would seek out unfiltered customer reviews instead.

nodes.label: online-review-volume-trustor
nodes.description: Trusts products with a high volume of reviews (e.g., 15,000) over those with a slightly higher rating but few reviews (e.g., 120).
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] You're comparing body washes online and see these ratings: Brand A: 4.2 stars, 15,000 reviews, ₹279. Brand B: 4.8 stars, 120 reviews, ₹329. Which do you trust more and buy? A: Brand A with 4.2 stars and 15,000 reviews. Higher review count gives more reliable feedback.

[SYNTHETIC] You're browsing Nykaa and find two products at ₹349. One has 2,400 ratings with a 4.1 average. The other has 85 ratings with a 4.8 average. Which one do you click on first? A: Click on the one with 2,400 ratings and 4.1 average first.
nodes.predictive_value: A new product launch with only a few dozen 'early bird' reviews will be passed over in favor of established products with thousands of data points.

nodes.label: clinical-evidence-requirer
nodes.description: Values clinical-grade, lab-tested formulations and is willing to pay a 20-40% premium for them, but needs to 'see the clinical study results first' rather than just a marketing claim.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What is the maximum premium (%) you would pay for a product that aligns with your values? Rate each separately: A: {"Clinical-grade, lab-tested formulation":"20_40"}

[BRANCH] Q: What if your regular ₹299 body wash launched a 'dermatologist-recommended' version at ₹399 with the same ingredients but clinical testing certification? Would you upgrade? A: I'd need to see the clinical study results first.
nodes.predictive_value: A claim of 'dermatologically tested' without a link to or summary of the study will be ignored as empty marketing.

nodes.label: budget-constrained-under-100
nodes.description: Typically spends 'Under_100' on a single body wash purchase and considers 'cheapest' a primary positive attribute of their current product.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How much do you typically spend on a single body wash purchase? A: Under_100
[REAL] What do you like MOST about your current body wash? (Top 1-2 things) A: Cheapest
nodes.predictive_value: A body wash concept priced above ₹250 will be immediately perceived as a premium purchase requiring significant extra justification.

nodes.label: premium-for-proven-benefits-spender
nodes.description: While typically budget-constrained, is willing to pay 2x the price for tangible benefits like 'more fragrance', 'more cleansing', and 'less chemicals'.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What would a body wash need to offer for you to pay 2x your current price? A: Less chemicals, more cleansing, more fragrance
[REAL] What is the maximum premium (%) you would pay for a product that aligns with your values? ... A: {"Ayurvedic or traditional ingredients":"20_40"}
nodes.predictive_value: A communication focusing on superior fragrance longevity and ingredient purity (e.g., '98% natural origin') can successfully justify a higher price point.

nodes.label: international-brand-bias
nodes.description: Holds a 'Bias towards international brands' due to perceived lax certification standards in India ('getting certificates and approvals to launch products is too easy').
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How do you feel about Indian personal care brands vs. international brands? Do you have a preference or bias? A: Bias towards international brands – in India, getting certificates and approvals to launch products is too easy
nodes.predictive_value: A 'Made in Korea' or 'Swiss Formula' claim would carry more inherent credibility than an 'Proudly Indian' claim, all else being equal.

nodes.label: very-loyal-until-triggered
nodes.description: Self-describes as 'Very_loyal' to personal care brands, but will actively 'Look_alternatives' if price increases 30% or if a core need (like fragrance longevity) is unmet.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How loyal are you to personal care brands? A: Very_loyal
[REAL] If a brand you liked raised its price by 30%, what would you do? A: Look_alternatives
[REAL] Describe the last time you tried a completely new body wash brand. What triggered the switch? Were you satisfied? A: Old one lacked strong fragrance – main reason for switching.
nodes.predictive_value: While they won't switch on a whim, a competitor's ad that directly highlights superior fragrance longevity can successfully trigger a reevaluation of their current loyal brand.

nodes.label: quick-commerce-for-urgency
nodes.description: Uses quick commerce (Blinkit, Zepto) and prioritizes immediate need over savings when 'running out now', choosing 10-minute delivery over a delayed discount.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] Your regular body wash costs ₹299. During Amazon Great Indian Festival, it's available at ₹199, but delivery will take 5 days. On Blinkit, it's available at full price ₹299 with 10-minute delivery and you're almost out. What do you do? A: Buy on Blinkit at ₹299 for 10-minute delivery. Running out now means convenience outweighs saving ₹100 with delay.
[REAL] Where do you most often buy personal care products? Pick your top 3: A: ["amazon","quick_commerce","nykaa"]
nodes.predictive_value: Out-of-stock scenarios on quick commerce apps are a key vulnerability; they are prime for last-minute, high-convenience substitute products.

nodes.label: ingredient-list-scanner
nodes.description: Actively reads ingredient lists, looking for key terms like SLS and verifying claims like 'Paraben-Free' by checking the label.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] You notice your body wash has 'Paraben-Free' printed on it. What would you most likely do? A: Flip the bottle to verify in the ingredient list.

[SYNTHETIC] What if a body wash listed 'Aqua, Sodium Laureth Sulfate, Cocamidopropyl Betaine...' on the back label? Would you (A) understand most of the ingredients, (B) recognize a few key ones like SLS... A: Recognize a few key ones like SLS.
nodes.predictive_value: Product pages or packaging that prominently feature a clean, well-organized ingredient list with explanations will earn more trust and engagement.

nodes.label: social-proof-from-peers-not-influencers
nodes.description: Places higher weight on recommendations from close friends and colleagues over influencers, often researching their suggestions independently.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] Two influencers recommend different body washes... Whose recommendation are you more likely to act on? A: Influencer A (the skincare expert with detailed ingredient analysis).

[SYNTHETIC] You see a body wash ad on Instagram featuring a dermatologist... The same day, your close friend casually mentions they love a completely different body wash... Which do you choose? A: Choose the body wash recommended by my close friend.
nodes.predictive_value: A marketing campaign encouraging user-generated content and reviews from 'people like you' will be more effective than one featuring mega-influencers.

nodes.label: pragmatic-sustainability-valuator
nodes.description: Values sustainable packaging and social impact but assigns a finite monetary value (₹30-40), not willing to pay a full premium for these claims alone.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] You see a body wash at ₹349 that claims '100% recyclable packaging' and donates ₹10 per bottle to ocean cleanup. A nearly identical body wash without these claims costs ₹269. How much of the ₹80 difference do you feel is justified? A: About ₹30-40 – I value sustainability but not enough to pay ₹80 extra.
[REAL] What is the maximum premium (%) you would pay for a product that aligns with your values? ... A: {"Sustainable/eco-friendly packaging":"10_20"}
nodes.predictive_value: Sustainability claims must be paired with core product benefits (fragrance, efficacy) to justify any price premium; they are a tie-breaker, not a primary driver.

nodes.label: browsing-ritual-at-runout
nodes.description: When current product runs out, the default next step is to 'Browse_online' rather than auto-repurchase, treating it as an opportunity to reevaluate.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] When your current body wash/soap runs out, what is your typical next step? A: Browse_online
nodes.predictive_value: The moment they run out of product is a critical touchpoint for retargeting ads and 'Customers who viewed this also bought' recommendations.

nodes.label: multi-platform-price-comparer
nodes.description: Checks prices across Amazon, Nykaa, and quick commerce before buying, and will switch platforms for a better deal if not in urgent need.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] You're in a Zepto ordering screen and see a body wash from a brand that's popular in South Korea but just entered India... Your regular body wash has 4.2 stars with 15,000+ reviews. Which do you order for tonight's delivery? A: Order my regular body wash with 15,000+ reviews, more reliable feedback.

[SYNTHETIC] You're browsing body wash on Nykaa and find a brand you like, but you notice the same product is ₹50 cheaper at your local DMart. Would you drive to DMart to save ₹50, or buy it on Nykaa right now? A: Drive to DMart to save ₹50.
nodes.predictive_value: Dynamic pricing or platform-exclusive discounts can successfully channel their purchase to a specific retailer.

nodes.label: self-care-ritual-identifier
nodes.description: Views bathing/grooming as a 'Self_care_ritual', indicating an emotional and personal investment in the experience beyond basic hygiene.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How would you describe your relationship with bathing/grooming? A: Self_care_ritual
nodes.predictive_value: Ad creative that frames body wash as a moment of daily renewal and personal pampering will resonate more than purely functional 'cleansing' messaging.

nodes.label: texture-and-feel-optimizer
nodes.description: Has specific preferences for 'Lightweight' texture and 'Moisturized' post-wash feeling, and values lather as 'Nice_to_have'.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What texture do you prefer in a body wash? A: Lightweight
[REAL] What is your ideal post-wash skin feeling? A: Moisturized
[REAL] How important is lather/foam to your body wash experience? A: Nice_to_have
nodes.predictive_value: Product demonstrations or reviews focusing on skin feel and lightweight, non-sticky texture will be key persuasive elements.

nodes.label: minimalist-packaging-aesthetic-preference
nodes.description: Prefers packaging that looks 'Minimalist', but explicitly rejects 'clinical/minimalist' in favor of 'Modern' design for their ideal product.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Do you prefer packaging that looks: A: Minimalist
[REAL] If you could design your PERFECT body wash from scratch, describe it... Packaging: Modern, not clinical/minimalist
nodes.predictive_value: Packaging design that is sleek and modern but avoids looking sterile or medical will have higher shelf/appeal.

nodes.label: ad-claims-resonance-responder
nodes.description: Lists 'ad_resonance' as a top-2 reason to switch body wash, indicating marketing messaging can directly influence reconsideration.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What would make you switch away from your current body wash? Pick your top 3: A: ["better_smell","ad_resonance"]
nodes.predictive_value: Highly targeted, benefit-forward ads (e.g., 'Tired of fading fragrance?') can successfully disrupt loyalty and prompt a new product search.

nodes.label: content-preference-ingredient-breakdown
nodes.description: Prefers 'Ingredient_breakdown' and 'before_after' content types when discovering new products, favoring detailed, evidence-based formats.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What type of content makes you most likely to try a new product? Pick top 2: A: Ingredient_breakdown,before_after
nodes.predictive_value: Long-form blog posts or videos deconstructing ingredients and showing visual results will be more effective than mood-based or purely aesthetic content.

nodes.label: peer-pressure-resistant
nodes.description: Unaffected by social pressure to use premium products; would 'laugh it off' if a friend mocked their 'basic' brand choice.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] During a video call with friends, someone notices your body wash in the background and makes a joke about it being a 'basic' brand. How do you react? A: Laugh it off — I don't care what they think about my body wash.

[SYNTHETIC] You're in a PG/hostel with shared bathrooms. You notice others are using premium body washes... You currently use a ₹150 Dove body wash. Would you: A: Feel no pressure — my choice works for me.
nodes.predictive_value: Luxury or status-based branding ('for those who know better') will likely backfire; they respond to efficacy and value, not social signaling.

nodes.label: calculated-discount-evaluator
nodes.description: Prefers straightforward '₹100 off' discounts over complex 'Buy 2 Get 1 Free' or cashback offers, favoring immediate price reduction.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] Imagine your body wash costs ₹299. Rank these discount presentations from most to least appealing: A: '₹100 off — now ₹199' B: '33% off' C: 'Buy 2 Get 1 Free' D: '₹100 cashback on Paytm' E: 'Free shipping (worth ₹50) + ₹50 off' A: A) ₹100 off — now ₹199, B) '33% off', E) 'Free shipping + ₹50 off', C) 'Buy 2 Get 1 Free', D) '₹100 cashback on Paytm'.
nodes.predictive_value: Simple, transparent discounting will drive faster conversion than multi-step promotions requiring future actions.

nodes.label: pragmatic-gift-receiver
nodes.description: Will gratefully receive a gifted body wash but feels no obligation to use it if it doesn't align with preferences; will stick with their own choice.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] You're at a family gathering during Diwali, and your aunt gifts you a premium Ayurvedic body wash set. You already have a body wash you love. What do you do? A: Thank her warmly but continue using your preferred body wash and regift or store the set.

[SYNTHETIC] Your mother insists that a particular Ayurvedic body wash... is better... You prefer an international brand... How do you handle this? A: Politely explain my preference and stick with my choice.
nodes.predictive_value: Gifting campaigns may introduce the product to them, but won't guarantee adoption unless it meets their specific criteria upon personal evaluation.

nodes.label: bundle-averse-unless-clear-value
nodes.description: Prefers buying single items and is hesitant about bundles or subscriptions unless they offer clear, upfront savings without commitment.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] A new D2C body wash brand offers you two options: Option A: ₹349 one-time purchase. Option B: ₹279/month subscription with auto-delivery every 45 days (cancel anytime). Which do you choose? A: Choose the ₹279/month subscription for convenience and slight saving, with option to cancel anytime.

[SYNTHETIC] You're on a checkout counter at Health & Glow. The cashier says: 'Add ₹79 more to your bill and get this travel-size body wash free.' Your current bill is ₹521. Do you: A: Decline — I don't want to spend more than planned.
nodes.predictive_value: Limited-time 'buy one, get one free' offers are less effective than permanent, per-unit price reductions or flexible subscriptions.

nodes.label: occasion-based-product-tiering
nodes.description: Segregates product use by occasion: uses premium body washes for important events (first dates, weddings) and daily drivers for routine use.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] Imagine you're on a first date and want to feel extra confident. Would you: A: Use a special/premium body wash you reserve for important occasions.

[SYNTHETIC] You're about to attend a wedding in two days and realize your current body wash doesn't leave a lasting fragrance. Would you: A: Rush-buy a new fragrant body wash specifically for the event.
nodes.predictive_value: Marketing that positions a body wash as a 'special occasion' or 'confidence-boosting' product can justify a higher price point for occasional use.

nodes.label: research-before-zepto-checkout
nodes.description: Even on quick commerce apps with limited details, will pause and open Amazon/Nykaa to read reviews before completing an urgent purchase of an unknown brand.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] You're on Blinkit ordering essentials and need body wash urgently. The app shows limited product details... Do you (A) order a familiar brand without further research, (B) pause and open Nykaa/Amazon to read reviews first, or (C) quickly Google the top options before ordering? A: Pause and open Nykaa/Amazon to read reviews first.
nodes.predictive_value: Product listings on quick commerce need to provide key trust signals (star rating, review count) within the app to prevent abandonment for research.

nodes.label: format-flexible-gel-preferrer
nodes.description: Uses both body wash and bar soap, choosing body wash for its superior fragrance. Prefers gel formulas but is open to new formats (oil, solid) with proof of efficacy.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Do you currently use body wash, bar soap, or both? What drives that choice? A: Use both. Bars are cheapest but less fragrance. Body wash has more fragrance – drives my choice

[SYNTHETIC] A new D2C brand launches a 'solid body wash bar'... Would you: (A) Order the solid bar immediately to try, (B) Wait for it to appear on Amazon/Nykaa with reviews... A: Wait for it to appear on Amazon/Nykaa with reviews.
nodes.predictive_value: Innovations in format (concentrates, bars) will be evaluated on their functional merits (fragrance, efficacy) rather than novelty alone.

nodes.label: skin-concern-body-odor-focused
nodes.description: Lists 'Body_odor' as their primary skin concern, making fragrance longevity and cleansing efficacy directly tied to a core need.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What specific skin concerns, if any, do you have? Select all that apply. A: Body_odor
nodes.predictive_value: Claims around 'all-day freshness', 'odor control', and 'deep cleansing' will be perceived as directly relevant and personally beneficial.

nodes.label: brand-ambivalence-post-discovery
nodes.description: Once a product's efficacy is proven through personal use, brand ownership (e.g., parent company) or controversies become secondary considerations.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] You discover that your favorite body wash brand is actually manufactured by the same parent company as a budget brand you consider inferior. Does this change how you feel about your favorite brand? A: No, the formulation is different so it doesn't matter.
[SYNTHETIC] A body wash brand you love gets called out for greenwashing... What do you do? A: Wait for the brand's official response before deciding.
nodes.predictive_value: Negative press about a brand's parent company or marketing practices will not cause an immediate boycott if the product itself continues to perform.

nodes.label: P03_T018
nodes.identity_summary: This consumer is a pragmatic yet aspirationally conscious urban professional who prioritizes practical value over prestige. They are fiercely rational in purchase decisions—methodically comparing prices, scrutinizing reviews, and verifying ingredient claims—but harbor a strong desire for long-lasting fragrance that their budget constraints often prevent. Their trust lies with clinical experts over social influencers, and while they show brand loyalty when satisfied, they will readily switch if a product fails their core criteria of effective cleansing and enduring scent at a competitive price.

nodes.label: Expert-Driven Rationalist
nodes.dimension: Emotional versus Rational Advertising Message Processing

nodes.label: reviews-as-primary-validation-filter
nodes.description: Consistently uses online review counts and star ratings as the first and most trusted filter for evaluating new products, prioritizing volume of feedback over isolated high ratings.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] When shopping online for personal care, what do you look at first? Pick your top 3: A: ["star_rating","review_count","price"]
[SYNTHETIC] 'Brand A: 4.2 stars with 15,000 reviews. Higher review count matters more to me than a slightly better rating with few reviews.'
[SYNTHETIC] 'I'd click the one with 2,400 ratings and 4.1 average first for more reliable feedback.'
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a new product concept, they would first check its rating and review volume on Amazon/Nykaa; a product with under 100 reviews would be dismissed regardless of other claims.

nodes.label: price-to-value-optimizer-not-minimizer
nodes.description: Seeks the optimal balance between cost and perceived efficacy, not just the cheapest option, and will pay a modest premium for demonstrable benefits like stronger fragrance or proven ingredients.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] 'What would a body wash need to offer for you to pay 2x your current price? A: Less chemicals, more cleansing, more fragrance'

[REAL] 'What is the maximum premium (%) you would pay for a product that aligns with your values? Rate each separately: A: {"Ayurvedic or traditional ingredients":"20_40","Clinical-grade, lab-tested formulation":"20_40"}'
[SYNTHETIC] 'Buy two mid-range body washes at ₹250 each to last the full 3 months for balance between quality and cost.'
nodes.predictive_value: A price increase of 20-30% would be accepted if clearly linked to a tangible improvement in fragrance longevity or ingredient quality, but a 50% increase without justification would trigger a search for alternatives.

nodes.label: all-day-fragrance-as-non-negotiable-goal
nodes.description: Consistently expresses dissatisfaction with current products for lacking 'strong fragrance' and explicitly desires scent that lasts 'all day,' treating this as a primary purchase driver.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] 'Describe the last time you tried a completely new body wash brand. What triggered the switch? Were you satisfied? A: Old one lacked strong fragrance – main reason for switching. Still not satisfied after switching'
[REAL] 'How long should a body wash fragrance last on your skin? A: All_day'
[REAL] 'If you could design your PERFECT body wash from scratch, describe it... A: Fragrance: Should last entire day'
nodes.predictive_value: An ad claiming '72hr freshness' or 'long-lasting scent' would immediately capture their attention and be a primary reason for consideration, even over other skin benefits.

nodes.label: clinical-expert-trust-over-influencer-hype
nodes.description: Deeply trusts dermatologists and clinical recommendations, viewing them as credible, while dismissing lifestyle influencers as potentially inauthentic 'pushers' of products.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] 'How much do you trust the following sources for personal care product information? Rate each 1-5: A: {"Dermatologists or skin doctors":5,"Instagram or YouTube influencers":4}'

[REAL] 'Is there something specific about seeing products on Instagram or YouTube that makes you hesitate to purchase them directly from there? A: Influencers push products forcefully for likes and views'
[BRANCH] 'Influencer A (skincare expert, 50K followers)... would influence me more... a skincare expert's opinion carries weight, especially since I trust dermatologists.'
nodes.predictive_value: A product endorsed by a named dermatologist or bearing a 'dermatologically tested' claim would significantly raise their purchase intent, whereas a celebrity or mega-influencer campaign would be met with skepticism.

nodes.label: ingredient-list-scrutinizer-for-key-red-flags
nodes.description: Actively reads ingredient lists not for deep understanding but to spot and verify specific claims (like 'Sulfate Free') and check for known harsh chemicals, using it as a trust verification step.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] 'Which certifications or labels influence your purchase? Select all that apply. A: Sulfate_free,organic,ayurvedic'
[SYNTHETIC] 'If a body wash listed 'Aqua, Sodium Laureth Sulfate...' on the back label? Would you... A: B) Recognize a few key ones like SLS'
[SYNTHETIC] 'You notice your body wash has 'Paraben-Free' printed on it. What would you most likely do? A: B) Flip the bottle to verify in the ingredient list'
nodes.predictive_value: A front-label claim of 'Natural' or 'Sulfate-Free' will prompt them to immediately check the ingredient list; inconsistency here would break trust and lead to product rejection.

nodes.label: multi-platform-price-comparison-ritual
nodes.description: Before any purchase, habitually cross-checks prices across Amazon, Nykaa, and quick commerce apps, valuing the best deal over channel loyalty.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] 'Where do you most often buy personal care products? Pick your top 3: A: ["amazon","quick_commerce","nykaa"]'
[SYNTHETIC] 'Search for my usual brand on another app to see if it's cheaper before deciding.'
[SYNTHETIC] 'Allocate 30 minutes across: 10 min comparing prices across Amazon, Nykaa, Blinkit.'
nodes.predictive_value: A product available exclusively on a brand's website at full price will be bypassed in favor of finding it on a marketplace where they can compare prices and read reviews.

nodes.label: pragmatic-loyalty-abandoned-if-core-need-fails
nodes.description: Describes self as 'Very_loyal' but loyalty is conditional on the product delivering on core needs (fragrance, cleansing); will swiftly switch if it disappoints, without sentimental attachment.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] 'How loyal are you to personal care brands? A: Very_loyal'
[REAL] 'Describe the last time you tried a completely new body wash brand... Old one lacked strong fragrance – main reason for switching. Still not satisfied after switching'
[REAL] 'If a brand you liked raised its price by 30%, what would you do? A: Look_alternatives'
nodes.predictive_value: While they may repurchase a satisfactory product, a single failure in fragrance longevity or a significant price hike will break the loyalty loop and trigger an active search for replacements.

nodes.label: bias-towards-international-brand-perceived-rigor
nodes.description: Prefers international brands due to a perception that regulatory and certification standards are more stringent and trustworthy than in India.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] 'How do you feel about Indian personal care brands vs. international brands? Do you have a preference or bias? A: Bias towards international brands – in India, getting certificates and approvals to launch products is too easy'
[SYNTHETIC] 'Distribute trust points as: International premium brands 4, Pharmacy/dermat brands 3...'
[SYNTHETIC] 'Rank preference at store: 1. Well-known international brand, 2. Popular Indian brand, 3. Store-brand option.'
nodes.predictive_value: A new product from an established international parent company (e.g., L'Oréal, P&G) would get an automatic credibility boost over an unknown Indian D2C brand, even at a higher price point.

nodes.label: bathing-as-functional-self-care-ritual
nodes.description: Views bathing as a non-negotiable 'Self_care_ritual' focused on hygiene and feeling refreshed, not as a luxurious or sensorial indulgence.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] 'How would you describe your relationship with bathing/grooming? A: Self_care_ritual'
[REAL] 'Walk me through your typical bathing routine step by step...' (lists functional steps)
[SYNTHETIC] 'Use bar soap from family stash until salary comes.' (prioritizes function over format when needed)
nodes.predictive_value: Marketing focused on 'spa-like luxury' or 'extreme pampering' would resonate less than messages about 'effective cleansing,' 'all-day freshness,' and 'reliable daily ritual.'

nodes.label: social-perception-consciousness-in-scent-choice
nodes.description: Explicitly chooses scents based on 'how others will react' rather than personal preference, indicating grooming is partially a social performance.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] 'When you're choosing a scent, do you prioritize how it makes you feel or how you think others will react to it? A: How others will react'
[REAL] 'How much do you care about how you smell to others throughout the day? Is grooming more about feeling good yourself, or about how others perceive you, or both? A: Both'
nodes.predictive_value: Ads showing positive social reactions (e.g., colleagues noticing freshness, compliments from friends) will be more effective than ads focusing solely on personal sensory enjoyment.

nodes.label: delayed-gratification-over-impulse-discount
nodes.description: Will let a time-sensitive discount expire if they don't have an immediate need, preferring to buy only when the product is needed after proper research.
evidence_quotes: [BRANCH] 'I'd let the coupon expire. My current body wash still has two weeks left, and I don't like buying things I don't need immediately.'
[SYNTHETIC] 'Buy just one at ₹349 since I don't need the second one now and prefer not to stock up unnecessarily.'
nodes.predictive_value: Flash sales and 48-hour coupons are unlikely to trigger an impulse buy if they are not already at the 'replacement moment' in their consumption cycle.

nodes.label: natural-ayurvedic-as-preference-not-dogma
nodes.description: Values 'Ayurvedic' and 'natural' claims (rating 3/5) but defines them pragmatically (herbs, non-chemical elements, separate machinery) and does not pursue them at any cost.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] 'How important is it to you that a product is 'natural' or 'chemical-free'? A: {"rating":3,"explanation":"Utmost important"}'

[REAL] 'Could you share what ingredients or qualities you specifically look for that make a product feel 'natural' to you? A: 1. Made from Ayurvedic herbs 2. Separate machinery... 3. Non-chemical raw elements 4. Chemicals used within limits'
[REAL] 'What claims or product descriptions on a body wash would make you stop and read more? Pick top 3: A: ["72hr_freshness","ayurvedic"]'
nodes.predictive_value: An 'Ayurvedic' claim will make them pause and investigate, but it must be coupled with strong reviews and a reasonable price; they won't pay a large premium for it alone.

nodes.label: body-odor-as-primary-skin-concern-driver
nodes.description: Identifies 'Body_odor' as their only specific skin concern, directly linking product efficacy to combating odor through cleansing and lasting fragrance.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] 'What specific skin concerns, if any, do you have? Select all that apply. A: Body_odor'
nodes.predictive_value: Product concepts or ads that directly address 'fighting body odor,' 'all-day freshness,' or 'deep cleansing' will align perfectly with their stated top-of-mind concern.

nodes.label: independent-judgement-over-social-pressure
nodes.description: Reports being unaffected by social comments about using 'budget' products, trusting their own practical judgment over others' opinions.
evidence_quotes: [BRANCH] 'I'd stick with my ₹199 body wash without any second thoughts. As someone who's professionally sorted, I trust my own judgment and prioritize practicality over social comments.'
[SYNTHETIC] 'Laugh it off — I don't care what they think about my body wash.'
nodes.predictive_value: Marketing that relies on social shaming ('Don't be the one who...') or overt prestige signaling will backfire; they respond better to rational, benefit-driven messaging.

nodes.label: quick-commerce-for-urgency-not-discovery
nodes.description: Uses quick commerce (Blinkit, Zepto) for immediate replenishment of known items when out, not for exploring new brands due to limited product details.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] 'Do you use quick commerce (Blinkit, Zepto, Swiggy Instamart) for personal care? If yes, does the limited selection change what you buy? A: Yes, use quick commerce. Doesn't limit selection'
[SYNTHETIC] 'Pause and open Nykaa/Amazon to read reviews first' before buying on Blinkit.
[SYNTHETIC] 'Pick from Zepto’s limited range for instant delivery when I need it today.'
nodes.predictive_value: They are a poor target for new product launches exclusive to quick commerce; they will only buy familiar brands there. Discovery happens on Amazon/Nykaa.

nodes.label: ingredient-breakdown-content-as-trigger
nodes.description: Specifically seeks out and is persuaded by detailed 'ingredient_breakdown' content that explains what each component does, valuing transparency and education.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] 'What type of content makes you most likely to try a new product? Pick top 2: A: Ingredient_breakdown,before_after'
[REAL] 'Who do you follow or trust in the personal care space on social media? Name specific ones and what makes you trust them. A: Fit Tuber – covers every aspect of product'
[SYNTHETIC] 'Choose the detailed ingredient breakdown with percentages.' over a brand video.
nodes.predictive_value: A long-form blog post or video deconstructing the formula's science for Indian skin/hard water would be highly engaging and build trust more effectively than a glossy brand film.

nodes.label: trial-size-as-de-risking-mechanism
nodes.description: Prefers to try new products via small sachets or travel sizes before committing to a full bottle, using it as a low-cost risk mitigation strategy.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] 'Only try if they offer a 50ml trial size.'
[SYNTHETIC] 'Choose the Skin Discovery Kit with 7 sachets for ₹299.' to test variants.
[SYNTHETIC] 'Buy the ₹99 mini from YouTuber review' as most likely trial scenario.
nodes.predictive_value: Offering an affordable trial size (₹99-₹199) would dramatically increase their likelihood of trying a new, higher-priced brand, converting curiosity into a considered trial.

nodes.label: floral-sweet-gourmand-scent-gravitation
nodes.description: Naturally gravitates towards 'Floral' and 'sweet_gourmand' fragrance families, seeking a 'floral + men-specific mix' as an ideal.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] 'What fragrance families do you naturally gravitate toward? Pick your top 2: A: Floral,sweet_gourmand'
[REAL] 'Name a specific product whose fragrance you loved... Scent was floral + men-specific mix'
nodes.predictive_value: A new product launch with a 'jasmine & sandalwood' or 'vanilla & rose' descriptor would inherently appeal more than citrus, woody, or aquatic scents.

nodes.label: packaging-minimalism-with-modern-touch
nodes.description: Prefers 'Minimalist' packaging but not clinical; desires something 'Modern, not clinical/minimalist' for their ideal, indicating a nuanced appreciation for aesthetic.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] 'Do you prefer packaging that looks: A: Minimalist'
[REAL] 'If you could design your PERFECT body wash from scratch... Packaging: Modern, not clinical/minimalist'
nodes.predictive_value: Packaging that is sleek, clean, and modern (like Fiama or some D2C brands) will be preferred over overly ornate, overly clinical (pharma-style), or garishly colored designs.

nodes.label: research-before-any-physical-store-purchase
nodes.description: Even when in a physical store, will pull out their phone to check reviews and compare prices online before making a final purchase decision.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] 'Check the Shark Tank brand's reviews on my phone before deciding.' in store.
[SYNTHETIC] 'Scan the product in Nykaa app and read reviews right there' before buying in-store.
[SYNTHETIC] 'Wait patiently for the pages to load' to research in-store despite slow data.
nodes.predictive_value: In-store displays and promotions alone are insufficient to drive a purchase; they must be supported by strong, accessible online social proof visible via a quick phone search.

nodes.label: hard-water-awareness-but-not-driver
nodes.description: Acknowledges that water hardness affects skin feel but has 'Never thought of this' in relation to product choice, though is somewhat interested in formulations for it.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] 'How does your water quality affect your product choices, if at all? A: Never thought of this'
[REAL] 'Have you ever noticed any difference in how your skin feels after showering at home compared to somewhere else, like on vacation? A: Yes. Mainly due to difference in water hardness'
[REAL] 'Would you be interested in a body wash specifically formulated for Indian skin types and hard water conditions? A: {"rating":4,"explanation":""}'
nodes.predictive_value: A 'hard water formula' claim is a nice-to-have secondary benefit that can nudge consideration, but it will not override primary factors like price, fragrance, and core reviews.

nodes.label: professionally-sorted-personal-growth-mindset
nodes.description: Self-describes as 'Professionally sorted Personally Growing,' indicating confidence in their career judgment and an openness to self-improvement in personal life.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] 'How would you describe your personality in 3-5 words? A: Professionally sorted Personally Growing'
nodes.predictive_value: Marketing that aligns with a narrative of 'elevating your daily routine' or 'smart choices for the growing professional' would resonate with their self-perception.

nodes.label: low-trust-in-packaging-claims-alone
nodes.description: Distrusts 'Claims written on the product packaging' (rates it 1/5 for trust), requiring external validation from reviews or experts.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] 'How much do you trust the following sources for personal care product information? Rate each 1-5: A: {"Claims written on the product packaging":1}'
nodes.predictive_value: Front-of-pack claims like 'New!' or 'Best Ever!' will be ignored unless substantiated by third-party reviews or expert endorsements visible elsewhere.

nodes.label: preference-for-lightweight-moisturizing-feel
nodes.description: Prefers a 'Lightweight' texture that leaves skin feeling 'Moisturized,' not oily, dry, or squeaky clean.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] 'What texture do you prefer in a body wash? A: Lightweight'
[REAL] 'What is your ideal post-wash skin feeling? A: Moisturized'
nodes.predictive_value: Product concepts emphasizing a 'lightweight gel-cream' or 'moisturizing without heaviness' would match their desired sensory outcome better than those touting 'deep detox' or 'oil-based nourishment.'

nodes.label: direct-substitution-in-urgency-scenarios
nodes.description: When in urgent need and their preferred brand is unavailable, will pick the closest available alternative in type (e.g., 'moisturizing, gel-based') rather than experiment wildly.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] 'Pick the one closest to your usual brand's description (e.g., moisturizing, gel-based).' on Zepto when usual is out.
[SYNTHETIC] 'Pick the one that looks closest to your usual type (gel, cream, etc.)' at an airport store.
nodes.predictive_value: In out-of-stock situations, they are a prime target for retailer 'similar item' recommendations or shelf placements that group products by benefit (moisturizing gels) rather than brand.

nodes.label: social-recommendation-filtered-through-research
nodes.description: Values recommendations from friends but will not act on them blindly; instead, uses them as a signal to begin independent research on review platforms.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] 'How much do you trust... Friends and family: 4'
[SYNTHETIC] 'Look it up on Amazon/Nykaa and read reviews from strangers too.' after friends recommend.
[SYNTHETIC] 'Ask them and then research if it suits your skin type too.' after partner's skin improves.
nodes.predictive_value: A 'Recommended by a friend' badge on an e-commerce listing would be a positive signal, but they would still scroll to the reviews section before adding to cart.

nodes.label: P03_T019
nodes.identity_summary: A professionally focused, frugal, and fragrance-driven consumer who prioritizes scent longevity and daily skin feel. While strongly influenced by online reviews and dermatologist recommendations, they maintain a cautious optimism towards new brands, always cross-checking claims against community feedback. Their purchase decisions are methodical—balancing cost against proven ingredient benefits—and they show a pragmatic loyalty to brands that deliver on core promises of lasting fragrance and skin compatibility.

nodes.label: Evening Leisure Receptive
nodes.dimension: Contextual Sensitivity to Ad Exposure Environment

nodes.label: fragrance-longevity-obsessed
nodes.description: Mentions improving or ensuring 'longer-lasting fragrance' or 'fragrance longevity' as a desired improvement for four out of five specific concepts.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] I want body washes with longer-lasting fragrance...
[SURVEY] I would improve it by... ensuring the fragrance lasts longer...
[SURVEY] I would improve the fragrance longevity and ensure the product is available at my usual stores...
[SURVEY] ...ensure the personalized fragrance lasts longer throughout the day...
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a concept that highlights '24-hour scent' or 'fragrance that lasts all day', this person would rate relevance and appeal significantly higher.

nodes.label: methodical-online-reviewer
nodes.description: Systematically checks multiple platforms (Amazon, YouTube, Reddit) for product reviews, prioritizing star ratings and review counts before purchasing.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Do you read online reviews before buying personal care products? Where? A: Amazon,youtube,reddit
[REAL] When shopping online for personal care, what do you look at first? Pick your top 3: A: star_rating,review_count,price
[SYNTHETIC] If you had to allocate 100 points across these factors that influence your body wash choice... A: Online reviews (30), Own past experience (40)

[SYNTHETIC] You're browsing Nykaa and find your regular body wash at ₹349. There's a combo offer: buy 2 for ₹549 (saving ₹149). You only need one right now. What do you do? A: Buy just one at ₹349 since I only need one now.
nodes.predictive_value: When encountering a new product page, this person would immediately scroll to the reviews section and compare ratings across platforms before considering purchase.

nodes.label: price-anchored-to-dettol
nodes.description: Mentally anchors body wash value to affordable mass-market brands like Dettol, rarely spending above ₹250 and feeling dissatisfied when premium products don't justify their cost.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How much do you typically spend on a single body wash purchase? A: Under_100
[REAL] What is the most you have ever paid for a body wash? What was it and was it worth it? A: Arata – ₹250. Not worth it
[REAL] If you could design your PERFECT body wash from scratch... Price: Affordable for Indian middle class (similar to Dettol pricing)

[SYNTHETIC] Imagine two body washes are identical in ingredients and fragrance. One is packaged in a sleek, minimalist bottle and priced at ₹399. The other is in a basic plastic bottle priced at ₹249. Both are 250ml. Which do you buy? A: Buy the basic plastic bottle at ₹249 over the sleek ₹399 one.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a premium body wash priced above ₹300, this person would immediately compare it to Dettol's price point and require exceptional fragrance or ingredient claims to justify the difference.

nodes.label: dermatologist-trust-above-all
nodes.description: Places highest trust in dermatologist recommendations over influencers, family, or brand claims, seeking clinical validation for product efficacy.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How much do you trust the following sources for personal care product information? Rate each 1-5: A: Dermatologists or skin doctors:5
[SYNTHETIC] If you could only get body wash recommendations from ONE source for the next year, which would you choose? A: A dermatologist you follow on Instagram.

[SYNTHETIC] A body wash brand launches a campaign featuring a Bollywood celebrity you admire versus a campaign featuring real Indian dermatologists explaining the science. Which campaign makes you more likely to buy? A: The dermatologist campaign — I trust expert credibility more.

[BRANCH] You see two influencers you follow reviewing body washes on the same day... Who influences your purchase more? A: I'd weigh both, but probably lean towards Influencer A if the clinical claims are backed by reviews or certifications.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown an ad featuring a dermatologist explaining pH balance or clinical testing, this person would be significantly more likely to investigate the product compared to celebrity-endorsed alternatives.

nodes.label: influencer-skeptic-but-engaged
nodes.description: Follows and consumes influencer content (like Fit Tuber) for information but distrusts overt promotions, preferring authentic ingredient breakdowns over sponsored praise.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Who do you follow or trust in the personal care space on social media? Name specific ones and what makes you trust them. A: Fit Tuber – covers every aspect of product

[REAL] Is there something specific about seeing products on Instagram or YouTube that makes you hesitate to purchase them directly from there? A: Influencers push products forcefully for likes and views
[REAL] Have you ever bought a personal care product because you saw it on social media? Describe what happened. A: No, never
[SYNTHETIC] Two influencers recommend different body washes... Whose recommendation are you more likely to act on? A: More likely to act on the skincare expert's recommendation if backed by reviews.
nodes.predictive_value: When encountering influencer content, this person would watch for ingredient analysis but immediately disengage if the video feels overly promotional or lacks critical perspective.

nodes.label: ayurvedic-claims-responsive
nodes.description: Actively seeks Ayurvedic certifications and traditional ingredient claims, willing to pay a 20-40% premium for authentic herbal formulations.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What claims or product descriptions on a body wash would make you stop and read more? Pick top 3: A: ayurvedic
[REAL] What is the maximum premium (%) you would pay for a product that aligns with your values? Rate each separately: A: Ayurvedic or traditional ingredients: 20_40
[REAL] Could you share what ingredients or qualities you specifically look for that make a product feel 'natural' to you? A: 1. Made from Ayurvedic herbs
[SYNTHETIC] A new Ayurvedic body wash brand sends you a free sample with a QR code linking to a 10-minute video about their sourcing from Kerala. Would you... A: Scan but skim for 1-2 minutes.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown packaging with AYUSH certification or traditional herb mentions (neem, tulsi), this person would pause to verify ingredient lists and consider paying above their usual price range.

nodes.label: international-brand-bias
nodes.description: Prefers international brands over Indian ones due to perceived stricter quality control and certification processes, distrusting easier Indian approvals.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How do you feel about Indian personal care brands vs. international brands? Do you have a preference or bias? A: Bias towards international brands – in India, getting certificates and approvals to launch products is too easy

[SYNTHETIC] Imagine two body washes are identical in ingredients and fragrance. One is packaged in a sleek, minimalist bottle and priced at ₹399. The other is in a basic plastic bottle priced at ₹249. Both are 250ml. Which do you buy? A: Buy the basic plastic bottle at ₹249 over the sleek ₹399 one.

[SYNTHETIC] You're at a Reliance Smart Bazaar and notice your regular body wash is out of stock... Rank them 1-3 in order of preference. A: 1. Well-known international brand, 2. Popular Indian brand, 3. Store-brand option
nodes.predictive_value: When comparing two similar products, this person would instinctively lean toward the international brand unless the Indian brand demonstrates superior ingredient transparency or certification.

nodes.label: routine-loyalist-with-exit-criteria
nodes.description: Maintains high brand loyalty but has specific triggers for switching (fragrance fade, price hikes, better claims) and will methodically research alternatives when criteria are met.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How loyal are you to personal care brands? A: Very_loyal
[REAL] If a brand you liked raised its price by 30%, what would you do? A: Look_alternatives
[REAL] What would make you switch away from your current body wash? Pick your top 3: A: better_smell, ad_resonance

[BRANCH] A body wash brand you've never tried sends you a ₹150 discount coupon... Do you: A: I'd let the coupon expire. My current body wash still has two weeks left... I prefer to stick to my routine and only purchase when I'm out.
nodes.predictive_value: When a trusted brand changes formulation or raises prices, this person wouldn't switch impulsively but would begin a deliberate search for alternatives meeting their fragrance and price criteria.

nodes.label: ingredient-label-scanner
nodes.description: Regularly checks ingredient lists for key terms (SLS, parabens) and Ayurvedic herbs, but doesn't deeply understand all chemical components.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] When buying any personal care product, rank these factors from most to least important: A: ingredients ranked 6th
[SYNTHETIC] You notice your body wash has 'Paraben-Free' printed on it. What would you most likely do? A: Flip the bottle to verify in the ingredient list
[SYNTHETIC] What if a body wash listed 'Aqua, Sodium Laureth Sulfate, Cocamidopropyl Betaine...' on the back label? Would you... A: Recognize a few key ones like SLS
[REAL] Could you share what ingredients or qualities you specifically look for that make a product feel 'natural' to you? A: 1. Made from Ayurvedic herbs
nodes.predictive_value: When examining new packaging, this person would immediately flip to the ingredient list, scanning for familiar red flags (SLS) and desirable herbs before reading marketing claims.

nodes.label: quick-commerce-convenience-seeker
nodes.description: Regularly uses quick commerce apps (Blinkit, Zepto) for immediate needs but cross-checks products on Amazon/Nykaa for reviews before purchasing.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Where do you most often buy personal care products? Pick your top 3: A: amazon,quick_commerce,nykaa
[REAL] Do you use quick commerce (Blinkit, Zepto, Swiggy Instamart) for personal care? If yes, does the limited selection change what you buy? A: Yes, use quick commerce. Doesn't limit selection

[SYNTHETIC] You're browsing body wash on Zepto and notice your usual brand now shows 'Price increased: was ₹249, now ₹299.'... What's your most likely reaction? A: Search other apps to see if usual brand is cheaper before deciding
[SYNTHETIC] You're on Blinkit ordering essentials and need body wash urgently... Do you: A: Pause and open Nykaa/Amazon to read reviews first
nodes.predictive_value: When needing body wash urgently, this person would open quick commerce apps but simultaneously check review platforms, potentially delaying purchase for better information.

nodes.label: minimalist-packaging-preference
nodes.description: Prefers simple, minimalist packaging design but won't pay extra for it, valuing function over aesthetic appeal.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Do you prefer packaging that looks: A: Minimalist
[REAL] If you could design your PERFECT body wash from scratch... Packaging: Modern, not clinical/minimalist
[SYNTHETIC] If you had to allocate 100 points across these factors... A: Packaging 3 points
[SYNTHETIC] A body wash brand collaborates with a popular Indian fashion designer for a limited-edition packaging... Do you: A: Skip it — I'm paying for the product, not the bottle.
nodes.predictive_value: When presented with overly ornate or gimmicky packaging, this person would perceive it as marketing fluff and focus more on ingredient lists and price.

nodes.label: social-proof-driven-by-close-circles
nodes.description: Values recommendations from trusted friends/family over broad popularity, but still verifies with independent research.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How much do you trust the following sources for personal care product information? Rate each 1-5: A: Friends and family:4

[SYNTHETIC] Rank these five purchase triggers from most to least likely to make you buy a body wash you haven't tried before: A: 1) Dermatologist's YouTube recommendation 2) Bestseller tag on Amazon 3) Friend's bathroom 4) Flash sale on Nykaa 5) Free sample sachets
[SYNTHETIC] Two friends independently recommend two different body wash brands... Whose recommendation carries more weight? A: I'd look up both brands online before deciding
[SYNTHETIC] A colleague at work casually mentions your body wash is 'the budget option'... How does this affect your next purchase? A: I'd be curious and look up the ₹499 option online
nodes.predictive_value: When a close friend recommends a product, this person would note it down and research it thoroughly rather than buying immediately on trust alone.

nodes.label: hard-water-solution-curious
nodes.description: Noticed skin differences due to water hardness and is interested in products formulated for Indian water conditions, but wants enhanced fragrance as part of the solution.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Have you ever noticed any difference in how your skin feels after showering at home compared to somewhere else, like on vacation? A: Yes. Mainly due to difference in water hardness
[REAL] Would you be interested in a body wash specifically formulated for Indian skin types and hard water conditions? A: rating:4

[REAL] What aspect of such a formulation makes you somewhat interested, and is there anything specific you wish it could address for you? A: Products claiming Indian-skin formulation are showing results. Want more fragrance
[SYNTHETIC] During monsoon season, a brand launches a body wash specifically for humid weather... Would you: A: Try a small/travel size first
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a body wash claiming 'hard water compatible' or 'mineral deposit removal', this person would check if it also offers lasting fragrance before considering purchase.

nodes.label: cautious-novelty-adopter
nodes.description: Open to trying new products (rated 4/5 comfort) but requires multiple validation points (reviews, certifications, samples) before committing, especially for premium prices.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How comfortable are you trying new brands or products you haven't used before? A: 4
[SYNTHETIC] A new D2C body wash brand launches in India... at what stage would you consider buying? A: After it gets listed on Amazon/Nykaa with 100+ reviews
[SYNTHETIC] A body wash brand offers a 'satisfaction guarantee'... Does the guarantee change your willingness to try it? A: Maybe — I'd read the fine print first before trusting it
[BRANCH] A body wash brand you've never tried sends you a ₹150 discount coupon... A: I'd let the coupon expire. My current body wash still has two weeks left...
nodes.predictive_value: When introduced to a innovative product (e.g., solid body wash bar), this person would wait for it to accumulate 100+ reviews on major platforms before considering a trial purchase.

nodes.label: sustainability-willing-at-moderate-premium
nodes.description: Values eco-friendly packaging and sustainability claims but only willing to pay 10-20% more, not sacrificing core product performance for green credentials.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What is the maximum premium (%) you would pay for a product that aligns with your values? Rate each separately: A: Sustainable/eco-friendly packaging: 10_20

[SYNTHETIC] You see a body wash at ₹349 that claims '100% recyclable packaging' and donates ₹10 per bottle to ocean cleanup. A nearly identical body wash without these claims costs ₹269. How much of the ₹80 difference do you feel is justified? A: About ₹30-40 — I value sustainability but not enough to pay full difference
[SYNTHETIC] If a body wash came as a powder that you mix with water... The brand claims it's more sustainable... Would you: A: Try it once if there's a sample but wouldn't commit upfront
nodes.predictive_value: When shown sustainable packaging claims, this person would appreciate them but reject the product if the price premium exceeds 20% without clear performance benefits.

nodes.label: bathing-as-self-care-ritual
nodes.description: Views bathing as a dedicated self-care ritual with specific product sequencing, not merely functional cleansing.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How would you describe your relationship with bathing/grooming? A: Self_care_ritual

[REAL] Walk me through your typical bathing routine step by step. What products do you use and in what order? A: 1. Hair wash – Shampoo: Havintha 2. Conditioner: Herbert Essentials 3. Body wash: Arata 4. Face wash: Garnier Men 5. Groin area – same body wash

[BRANCH] Describe how your daily activities and mood influence when you are most likely to pay attention to advertisements for body wash on social media... A: I'm definitely more receptive to ads when I'm relaxing at home in the evening, after work... it feels like part of my self-care ritual
nodes.predictive_value: When shown ads framing body wash as part of a wellness ritual or evening wind-down routine, this person would be more receptive and likely to engage.

nodes.label: clinical-claims-skeptic-until-verified
nodes.description: Interested in clinical and lab-tested claims but requires seeing study details or dermatologist validation, distrusting vague marketing language.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What is the maximum premium (%) you would pay for a product that aligns with your values? Rate each separately: A: Clinical-grade, lab-tested formulation: 20_40

[BRANCH] What if your regular ₹299 body wash launched a 'dermatologist-recommended' version at ₹399 with the same ingredients but clinical testing certification? Would you upgrade? A: I'd need to see the clinical study results first

[SYNTHETIC] You're browsing body wash on Amazon India and notice a product has a 'Clinically Tested' badge, but when you scroll down, there's no link to the actual clinical study... Meanwhile, another body wash... has a detailed blog post linked in the description explaining how each ingredient works... Which product do you add to cart? A: Add the one with detailed blog post explaining ingredients
nodes.predictive_value: When presented with clinical claims without accessible study data, this person would seek alternative products with transparent ingredient explanations.

nodes.label: multi-brand-portfolio-user
nodes.description: Regularly uses 3-4 different body wash brands simultaneously, rotating based on specific needs (cost, fragrance, skin feel) rather than sticking to one.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What body wash brand(s) do you currently use? List all. A: Arata, Fiama, Pears, Dettol
[REAL] Do you currently use body wash, bar soap, or both? What drives that choice? A: Use both. Bars are cheapest but less fragrance. Body wash has more fragrance – drives my choice
[SYNTHETIC] Think about the last 3 body washes you purchased. Were they: A: D (Two of one brand, one different)
nodes.predictive_value: When presented with a new variant from an existing brand in their portfolio, this person would consider it as a potential addition rather than a replacement for current products.

nodes.label: price-comparison-across-platforms
nodes.description: Systematically checks prices across Amazon, Nykaa, and quick commerce before purchasing, even for small savings.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] You're browsing body wash on Nykaa and find a brand you like, but you notice the same product is ₹50 cheaper at your local DMart. Would you drive to DMart to save ₹50, or buy it on Nykaa right now? A: Drive to DMart to save ₹50

[SYNTHETIC] You see your favorite body wash at three different price points: ₹299 on the brand's website, ₹319 on Amazon, ₹329 at your local supermarket. Where do you buy it? A: Buy from Amazon for free Prime delivery and trusted service despite slight price difference

[SYNTHETIC] During a Nykaa sale, you add a body wash to your cart but get distracted... The next day, you get a 'cart reminder' email with an extra 5% off. Do you: A: Check if it's cheaper elsewhere now
nodes.predictive_value: Before any purchase, this person would open multiple shopping apps/tabs to compare final prices including discounts and delivery charges.
